# Azgari Foundation

> Azgari Foundation is a Delaware 501(c)(3) public charity based in Austin, Texas (EIN 41-2896092, SAM.gov UEI U95WFYNFERH3). It provides tuition-free AI, digital skills, and entrepreneurship training to young adults ages 16–25 aging out of foster care, exiting the juvenile justice system, and other opportunity youth.

## Current program

- **AI Agency Business Owner Training** — six-month, fully online, tuition-free program. Participants build and launch their own AI-powered marketing agency: AI tools and workflow automation, business fundamentals, financial literacy, sales, and client delivery, with one-to-one mentorship.
- **Pilot cohort:** August 1, 2026 – January 30, 2027. Twelve seats. Applications close July 25, 2026 at 11:59 PM CT at https://azgari.org/#apply
- **Next cohort:** March 2027. Interest list: email fosteryouthinitiative@azgari.org
- **Eligibility:** ages 16–25; foster care alumni, juvenile-justice-involved, or opportunity youth; 15–20 hours/week commitment. Free to all participants.

## People & governance

Board of Directors: Azgari Lipshy (President & Director), Stephen Dugan, Dr. Md Enamul Karim, Khadijah Karim, Nasreen Ahmed, Rowshanara Moly.

- Azgari Lipshy — President & Director, Founder (https://www.linkedin.com/in/azgari-lipshy/)
- Stephen Dugan — Co-Founder, AI & Software Director
- Dr. Md Enamul Karim — Faculty Chair (Professor & Chair, Mathematics & Computer Science, Southern Arkansas University — https://web.saumag.edu/directory/contact/karim-md/)
- Khadijah Karim — AI Education Fellow (Carnegie Mellon University — https://tartanconnect.cmu.edu/bsa/leadership-team/)
- Rowshanara Moly — Instruction & Program Administration, Board Member
- Nasreen Ahmed — Clinical Support Lead, LPC-A (https://nasreenahmed.com/) — works as a counselor in Texas
- Plus extended volunteer faculty: small-business accounting, enterprise marketing, prospecting, administration.
- Bylaws, conflict-of-interest policy, code of conduct, and IRS determination letter available to funders on request.

## Track record

Since January 2026 the Foundation has delivered scholarship-funded entrepreneurship education while designing this pilot — eight scholarships awarded to date; two participants operate active businesses today as a direct result of the program, and three more are employed while building toward ownership.

## Partners

- Youth First Community — program delivery partner
- Stephen Braggs Youth Foundation — cohort co-pilot (Austin-area 501(c)(3) serving foster youth 13–22, founded by retired NFL player Stephen Braggs)

## Funding

The Foundation is structured to receive WIOA Youth Formula contracted services, Chafee Foster Care Independence Program grants, HUD YHDP and Continuum of Care awards, Texas Workforce Commission contracts, SBA community capital-access programs, foundation grants, and corporate/individual philanthropy. Graduate sponsorships are $8,000 (one graduate's first-year business operating costs). Donations: fosteryouthinitiative@azgari.org. Tax-deductible to the extent permitted by law.

## Vision

Long-term: a wellness-focused live-and-learn campus (housing, learning hall, greenhouse, kitchen, fitness) where opportunity youth spend up to a year building a business and a healthy life. Contingent on grant and capital funding; not operating today.

## Key facts

- 501(c)(3) status: IRC §170(b)(1)(A)(vi) public charity; IRS determination letter issued March 10, 2026, effective December 1, 2025
- Mailing address: 8 The Green, Suite R, Dover, DE 19901 · Operations: 1 Brazos St, Austin, TX 78701
